titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( (Winant) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Winant ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
60. Department’s 10719, December 26, 8 p.m.
ibid., p. 483. While awaiting my general instructions on the question of a zone of occupation in Austria I have had MoselyPhilip E. Mosely, Political Adviser to the United States Delegation to the European Advisory Commission. present to GousevFedor Tarasovich Gousev, Soviet Ambassador in the United Kingdom and Representative on the European Advisory Commission. and his assistant, Ivanov,Nikolai V. Ivanov, member of the Soviet Delegation to the European Advisory Commission. the American viewpoint in favor of taking the present GauPolitical district. boundaries of Vienna rather than the pre-1938 limits and of dividing Innere StadtInner City. district among the occupying powers.
Our viewpoint has been presented forcefully and in detail but it is too early to discover whether the Soviet insistence on the pre-1938 boundaries has been modified.

At tonight’s meeting of the European Advisory Commission MassigliRené Massigli, French Ambassador in the United Kingdom and Representative on the European Advisory Commission. explained that the French Government would want to station a token contingent in Vienna but not take a zone in the rest of Austria in view of its hope that a large part of the available French forces would be committed to the occupation of a zone in Germany. Please furnish paraphrase to Generals HilldringMaj. Gen. John H. Hilldring, Director of the Civil Affairs Division of the War Department. and Strong.Maj. Gen. George V. Strong, senior Army representative on the Joint Post-War Committee.
